   "source": [
    "# Spectrogram: Basics\n",
    "\n",
    "[![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/tatsuki-washimi/gwexpy/blob/main/docs/web/en/user_guide/tutorials/intro_spectrogram.ipynb)\n",
    "\n",
    "This notebook introduces the newly added `SpectrogramList` and `SpectrogramDict` classes in `gwexpy`.\n",
    "\n",
    "These classes are containers for managing multiple spectrogram data (`gwpy.spectrogram.Spectrogram`) collectively and performing batch operations (cropping, rebinning, plotting, etc.).\n",
    "They have a similar interface to `TimeSeriesList` / `TimeSeriesDict` which handle time series data, but are specialized for 2D data (time × frequency)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "de74dcce",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-05-08T05:58:57.075200Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-05-08T05:58:57.075004Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-05-08T05:59:01.944776Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-05-08T05:59:01.941365Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 4.877735,
     "end_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:01.946042+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-05-08T05:58:57.068307+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import warnings\n",
    "\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=UserWarning)\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=DeprecationWarning)\n",
    "\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy.noise.wave import gaussian, sine\n",
    "from gwexpy.spectrogram import SpectrogramDict, SpectrogramList"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "280868a0",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.006314,
     "end_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:01.985321+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:01.979007+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 1. Data Preparation\n",
    "\n",
    "First, let's create demo spectrogram data.\n",
    "Here, we generate time series data containing sine waves at different frequencies and convert them using the `spectrogram()` method."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "19b33bf8",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-05-08T05:59:02.062231Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-05-08T05:59:02.061840Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.074788Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.072342Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 1.020172,
     "end_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.075283+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:02.055111+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Spectrogram shape: (10, 65)\n",
      "Time range: [0.0 ... 20.0)\n",
      "Freq range: [0.0 ... 65.0)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Create sample data\n",
    "duration = 20  # seconds\n",
    "sample_rate = 128  # Hz\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create sine waves at 10Hz, 30Hz, 50Hz (+noise)\n",
    "noise_amp = 0.1\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "def make_signal(freq, name):\n",
    "    s = sine(duration=duration, sample_rate=sample_rate, frequency=freq, amplitude=1.0)\n",
    "    n = gaussian(duration=duration, sample_rate=sample_rate, std=noise_amp)\n",
    "    ts = s + n\n",
    "    ts.name = name\n",
    "    ts.override_unit(\"strain\")\n",
    "    return ts\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "ts1 = make_signal(10, \"Signal 10Hz\")\n",
    "ts2 = make_signal(30, \"Signal 30Hz\")\n",
    "ts3 = make_signal(50, \"Signal 50Hz\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Calculate spectrograms (stride=2s, fftlength=1s)\n",
    "# Specify nproc=1 for stability with serial execution\n",
    "spec1 = ts1.spectrogram(2, fftlength=1, overlap=0.5, nproc=1)\n",
    "spec2 = ts2.spectrogram(2, fftlength=1, overlap=0.5, nproc=1)\n",
    "spec3 = ts3.spectrogram(2, fftlength=1, overlap=0.5, nproc=1)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Spectrogram shape:\", spec1.shape)\n",
    "print(\"Time range:\", spec1.xspan)\n",
    "print(\"Freq range:\", spec1.yspan)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "9120599b",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.099693,
     "end_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.180385+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.080692+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 2. Basic Operations with SpectrogramList\n",
    "\n",
    "`SpectrogramList` behaves like a list, but can only store `Spectrogram` objects.\n",
    "You can pass a list during initialization or add items using the `append()` method."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "43285f98",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.209192Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.208872Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.216674Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.215795Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.015174,
     "end_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.217173+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.201999+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "List length: 3\n",
      "Items: ['Signal 10Hz', 'Signal 30Hz', 'Signal 50Hz']\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Create a list\n",
    "spec_list = SpectrogramList([spec1, spec2])\n",
    "\n",
    "# Append\n",
    "spec_list.append(spec3)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"List length: {len(spec_list)}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Items: {[s.name for s in spec_list]}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "72e9d3c6",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.053263,
     "end_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.275863+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.222600+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "### Batch Processing: Cropping Time Axis (`crop`)\n",
    "\n",
    "Performs batch cropping of the time axis for all spectrograms in the list.\n",
    "**Note:** Since spectrograms have large data volumes, operations like cropping may return new objects (copies) without modifying the original data."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "dfb48fa0",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.304862Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.304173Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.311650Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.311320Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.015208,
     "end_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.312443+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.297235+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Original range: [0.0 ... 20.0)\n",
      "Cropped range: [4.0 ... 14.0)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Crop to 5-15 second range\n",
    "cropped_list = spec_list.crop(5, 15)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Original range:\", spec_list[0].xspan)\n",
    "print(\"Cropped range:\", cropped_list[0].xspan)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "ae085291",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.005875,
     "end_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.323312+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.317437+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "### Batch Processing: Cropping Frequency Axis (`crop_frequencies`)\n",
    "\n",
    "If there's a compatible method in gwpy's `Spectrogram`, it will be used; otherwise, filtering is performed by specifying the frequency axis."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "049e6ff7",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {

    },

   },
   "outputs": [

     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Original yspan: [0.0 ... 65.0)\n",
      "Cropped yspan: [0.0 ... 40.0)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Crop to 0Hz - 40Hz range\n",
    "freq_cropped = spec_list.crop_frequencies(0, 40)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Original yspan:\", spec_list[0].yspan)\n",
    "print(\"Cropped yspan:\", freq_cropped[0].yspan)\n",
    "# The third element containing the 50Hz signal will also have out-of-range portions cut"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "## 3. Using SpectrogramDict\n",
    "\n",
    "`SpectrogramDict` is convenient when you want to manage spectrograms with names (keys)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 7,
   "id": "59ceffe0",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.632435Z",

     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.637417Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.635859Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.027653,
     "end_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.638713+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.611060+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Keys: ['low_freq', 'mid_freq', 'high_freq']\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "spec_dict = SpectrogramDict({\"low_freq\": spec1, \"mid_freq\": spec2, \"high_freq\": spec3})\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Keys:\", list(spec_dict.keys()))"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "## 4. Visualization (`plot`)\n",
    "\n",
    "Calling the `.plot()` method on a list or dictionary allows you to plot all contained spectrograms together.\n",
    "Internally, it uses `gwpy.plot.Plot`."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 8,
   "id": "136f11e6",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.678278Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-05-08T05:59:03.678111Z",

    },

   "source": [
    "# Batch plotting\n",
    "# Setting sharex=True, sharey=True shares axes for easier comparison\n",
    "plot = spec_dict.plot(figsize=(12, 8), sharex=True, sharey=True)\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "## 5. Conversion to Matrix Format and Deep Learning Integration\n",
    "\n",
    "Using `to_matrix()`, you can create a `SpectrogramMatrix` object with a 3D array `(Channels, Time, Frequency)` that stacks each spectrogram.\n",
    "\n",
    "This is very convenient when creating inputs for machine learning models (PyTorch, TensorFlow, etc.)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 9,
   "id": "f8eb967a",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-05-08T05:59:30.808653Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-05-08T05:59:30.808396Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-05-08T05:59:30.820255Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-05-08T05:59:30.819130Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.0229,
     "end_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:30.821050+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:30.798150+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Matrix Type: <class 'gwexpy.spectrogram.matrix.SpectrogramMatrix'>\n",
      "Matrix Shape: (3, 10, 65)\n",
      "  (N_channels, N_time_steps, N_freq_bins)\n",
      "Time axis shape: (10,)\n",
      "Freq axis shape: (65,)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "matrix = spec_list.to_matrix()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Matrix Type:\", type(matrix))\n",
    "print(\"Matrix Shape:\", matrix.shape)\n",
    "print(\"  (N_channels, N_time_steps, N_freq_bins)\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Accessing attributes\n",
    "print(\"Time axis shape:\", matrix.times.shape)\n",
    "print(\"Freq axis shape:\", matrix.frequencies.shape)"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "## Common Mistakes and Failure Modes\n",
    "\n",
    "- **Reading color intensity as absolute amplitude without checking settings**: different normalization, color limits, or log scaling choices can change the visual impression without changing the underlying data.\n",
    "- **Over-interpreting time or frequency localization**: `fftlength`, `stride`, and windowing create a resolution tradeoff. A narrow-looking feature in the image is not automatically narrow in the signal model.\n",
    "- **Comparing panels with unmatched axes**: if `sharex` / `sharey` or explicit ranges are inconsistent, visual differences may come from plotting choices rather than spectral structure.\n",
    "- **Treating cropped output as a new physical measurement**: `crop` and `crop_frequencies` only restrict the displayed or retained region; they do not regenerate the spectrogram with different estimation parameters.\n",
    "- **Feeding matrices into ML pipelines without tracking axis meaning**: `SpectrogramMatrix` stores `(Channels, Time, Frequency)`. Swapping axes silently can produce convincing but incorrect downstream results.\n",
    "- **Ignoring noise-floor context**: a bright band is not automatically significant. Interpret peaks relative to baseline structure, leakage, and the chosen spectrogram parameters.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "47ffc143",
   "metadata": {

     "duration": 0.008739,
     "end_time": "2026-05-08T05:59:30.976398+00:00",

    "## Summary\n",
    "\n",
    "This concludes the introduction to the basic usage of `SpectrogramList` and `SpectrogramDict`.\n",
    "Please make use of these classes when batch preprocessing large amounts of spectrogram data or creating comparison plots."
   ]
  }
